item.title: Luxury may be in the doldrums, but perfume passes the smell test
item.description: Fragrances are among the faster-growing areas of the beauty industry — though investing in them isn’t straightforward

item.title: Burnham catches a bit of luck with gilts
item.description: Global bond markets have rallied, easing pressure on UK’s prime minister-in-waiting

item.title: Mistrust between the Treasury and MoD is holding back UK defence
item.description: Resolving the impasse between the two departments is urgent

item.title: Greenspan and the important art of Fedspeak
item.description: Former Fed chair’s ambiguous communication style contrasted with his efforts to make the bank more transparent

item.title: I’ll always defend cyclists — but it’s anarchy out there
item.description: The righteous rage of the road can teach us a lot about our psychology

item.title: Shorts-wearers of the world, unite!
item.description: As summer temperatures soar, it makes no sense — sartorially or environmentally — to stick with long trousers

item.title: How will the ‘King of the North’ tax the wealth of the south?
item.description: Better-off Britons braced for tax rises at Andy Burnham’s first Budget

item.title: The £700 white burgundy was good. Better yet were the least expensive bottles
item.description: A blind tasting of top Chardonnays confirmed one of wine’s most cheering truths: price is no guarantee of pleasure

item.title: ‘Clever, laser-focused cooking’ — Jay Rayner reviews Teal by Sally Abé
item.description: The chef is no newcomer, but this small but perfectly formed new Hackney restaurant feels like her breakout

item.title: Ask a stylist: Is there a more modern way to cover up at the beach than a kaftan?
item.description: For coverage with a carefree feel, there is a vast choice of youthful, vibrant variants of this summer essential

item.title: Ill-timed deregulation is weakening bank resilience
item.description: Thoughtful, fundamental reform is needed in finance, not an industry checklist

item.title: What Kevin Warsh can learn from Alan Greenspan
item.description: The new Fed chair must learn from his late predecessor’s successes — and avoid his mistakes

item.title: The downsides of making $13tn of profit
item.description: Betting on US equity has been staggeringly lucrative for the rest of the world, but it is now far more exposed to a market crash

item.title: Walmart’s ad deal smartly puts its customers in the shopping basket
item.description: Retailer looks to monetise its deep well of consumer data and insights

item.title: Volkswagen’s mass lay-offs are a call to action for a sleepy EU
item.description: Carmaker’s move will ratchet up the pressure on European policymakers to slap restrictions and trade barriers on China

item.title: The hole in British politics
item.description: Andy Burnham must fix the widespread idea that government is not on our side

item.title: Your summer holiday is a retirement killer
item.description: The capitalised value of that all-inclusive fortnight will scare you

item.title: How quickly should a battered Venezuela restructure its debt?
item.description: The country is trying to seal a quick deal with bondholders in the face of two devastating earthquakes but some are concerned

item.title: Do we all need a hydration break?
item.description: Only the discipline of an enforced, ordered and joyless break can ensure we maximise the benefits of our work

item.title: The leadership change in US stocks
item.description: A market seeking direction

item.title: Bending Spoons tries out an odd kind of financial magic trick
item.description: Italian conglomerate is an emergency room for critically injured tech businesses where returns come from earnings alone

item.title: The shrinking arguments for degrowth
item.description: Economic growth isn’t everything for everyone, but it turns out it’s pretty close

item.title: My maxim for the best scented garden: ‘See it, sniff it, source it’
item.description: From philadelphus, or mock orange blossom, to dianthus from the ‘Scent First’ series — midsummer never smelt so sweet

item.title: Trump’s tests of the US justice system
item.description: For the time being, it is just about holding

item.title: VW’s Everllence sale shows even ailing carmakers have hidden charms
item.description: Deal suggests carmakers may have more value packed away in the trunk
